mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales and housing starts both rise, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when industrial production increases, specifically during recovery regimes, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation levels trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPI exceeds 255, contradicting the tightening expectation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in the majority of observed cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between GDP growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.1, or returns are negative in > 55% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and reduced corporate output, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where industrial production drops by more than 0.5 percent.
